summary: You must register with HMRC for Self Assessment as a sole trader once your self-employed income exceeds £1,000 in a tax year. Registration is free and must be completed by 5 October following the end of the tax year in which you started trading — missing this deadline can result in penalties.

item: COSHH (Control of Substances Hazardous to Health) Regulations 2002 Compliance
summary: Oven deep-cleaning involves caustic degreasers and strong chemicals that fall under COSHH Regulations 2002. You must carry out a written risk assessment for each chemical used, implement controls to prevent exposure, and wear appropriate PPE — this applies even if you are a sole trader with no employees.

If you transport any waste generated during cleaning jobs away from customers' homes (e.g. chemical residues, dirty water, used materials), you must register as a waste carrier with the Environment Agency. Operating without registration carries an unlimited fine.

There is no statutory requirement for sole traders to hold public liability insurance, but it is strongly advised for any business working inside customers' homes. It covers you against third-party claims for property damage or injury caused while on a client's premises — for example, damaging an oven or worktop during cleaning.

item: Making Tax Digital (MTD) for Income Tax — Sole Traders
summary: From April 2026, sole traders with income above £50,000 must keep digital records and submit quarterly updates to HMRC instead of a single annual return. If this business grows, you will need compatible software in place before the threshold is reached.
